task_completion.description: Open the built-in 'Fly Brain' sample in Fiji/ImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image window titled 'flybrain' (or similar) showing a 2-channel (red/green) confocal brain structure. The Fiji/ImageJ toolbar should be visible.

task_completion.description: Convert the image stack to 8-bit grayscale.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image window header should update to indicate '8-bit' and the image itself should appear in grayscale instead of the original red/green colors.

task_completion.description: Reslice the stack from the 'Top' using 1.0 pixel spacing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Reslice' dialog box showing 'Start at: Top' and 'Spacing: 1.0', followed by a new image window (often titled 'Reslice of flybrain') showing an XZ cross-section. The new window's header should indicate dimensions of 256x57 pixels.

task_completion.description: Create a Maximum Intensity Projection of the resliced view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'ZProjection' dialog box with 'Projection type: Maximum Intensity' selected, followed by a new image window titled 'MAX_Reslice...' showing a single 2D projection image (no Z-slider at the bottom of the window).

task_completion.description: Save the resulting projection image to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/side_view_projection.tif
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box saving the file as 'side_view_projection.tif' in the '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results' directory, or a file manager/terminal showing the successfully saved file at this path.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Fiji/ImageJ GUI to perform the image processing steps rather than running a script or terminal command.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with Fiji/ImageJ menus (e.g., Image > Type > 8-bit, Image > Stacks > Reslice, Image > Stacks > Z Project) and dialog boxes.

integrity.description: Verify the final image is a genuine result of the processing steps and not a downloaded or manually created dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final image should visually match a maximum intensity projection of a resliced brain stack (a distinct biological structure in grayscale), not a blank, random, or text-based image.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually interacted with the Fiji/ImageJ application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Fiji/ImageJ main toolbar and standard ImageJ image windows must be clearly visible in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Fly Brain' sample is a built-in Fiji/ImageJ sample accessible via File > Open Samples > Fly Brain. The original stack is 256x256 pixels with 57 Z-slices. Converting to 8-bit changes the image from a 2-channel (red/green) view to grayscale. Reslicing from the 'Top' creates an XZ orthogonal view. The final Maximum Intensity Projection should be a single 2D image (1 slice) with dimensions 256x57 pixels, saved as 'side_view_projection.tif' in '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/'.
